text.p: This is to bring to your notice that SER 001: Use of English is a 4-unit course, which is meant to be registered in the Harmattan Semester. However, it is a sessional course because the examination is taken at the end of the Harmattan Semester.

During the registration of course in the Harmattan Semester 2006/2007, some head of departments complained to the Department of English that their Part I students would not be able to register for SER 001 because it would make the number of units of the courses they register to overshoot the required maximum of 24 units. In order to accommodate such students, the Department decided to split the 4 units for SER 001, thereby allowing such students to register for 2 units in the Harmattan Semester and 2 units in the Rain Semester However, for those who were able to accommodate the 4 units, they would not need to register again in the Rain Semester.

It was however brought to our notice this semester that the course still reflects 4 units for the Rain semester. What this translates into is that those who registered for 2 units in the Harmattan and now register for the course in the Rain semester will now have a total of 6 units. This should not be so. The Department also learnt because of the inability of some students to resolve the issue of the number of units, they could not register for the course in the Rain Semester.

In view of the fact that some students who registered for the course in the Harmattan Semester could not register for it in the Rain Semester, the Department is requesting the Computer Centre to help to resolve this issue by automatically registering all those who registered 2 units in the Harmattan Semester, so that they can complete the required number of units for the course.
